purpose: Replace inferred placeholder descriptions for high-value operator and agent commands with curated descriptions, domains, and work gates.

overrides.description: Run a command by registry id through the metadata-aware command runner; preferred agent entrypoint over raw package-script spelunking.
List command registry entries with metadata filters for domain, work gate, lifecycle, group, and search text.
Summarize command registry groups, domains, and three-commandment work gates for surface compression.
Run the Kernel Sentinel automatic observation loop with bounded runtime, compact artifacts, trace identity, and maintenance cadence controls.
Run the lightweight Kernel Sentinel heartbeat path for freshness without expensive dream-state self-study.
Evaluate whether Kernel Sentinel evidence is fresh, bounded, and strong enough for release confidence.
Guard that verifies Kernel Sentinel has fresh evidence artifacts and is not relying on stale self-observation.
Start or attach to the Gateway runtime surface without treating Shell state as authority.
Read Gateway status and health projection for operator diagnostics without mutating runtime state.
Stop the Gateway runtime through the governed command path while preserving diagnostic output.
Validate Gateway interface policy: bounded route classes, forbidden raw state shapes, and projection-only payload contracts.
Guard the Gateway boundary membrane so external clients cannot bypass typed, bounded, auditable ingress.
Run the personal installation path for local operator setup using the public installer compatibility surface.
Validate Windows installer contracts, wrapper expectations, and locked-down execution-policy recovery behavior.
Run dependency security audit for known package vulnerabilities before release or risky dependency work.
Produce the release verdict from validation, proof-pack, Sentinel, and governance evidence.
Build or refresh release proof-pack artifacts used by release gates and operator review.
Gate release confidence on boundedness evidence such as memory, queue, stale-surface, and recovery budgets.
Read memory matrix status as a kernel-owned diagnostic rather than a Shell-owned cache mirror.
Run memory efficiency analysis to find retention, compaction, and representation-collapse opportunities.
Plan an explicit-path surgical commit with a temporary Git index, diff checks, and no mutation of the real staging area.
Verify Kernel Sentinel release-bridge repair evidence so stale observation state cannot masquerade as release truth.
Check Sentinel boundedness repair artifacts for compact, fresh diagnostics instead of unbounded self-observation noise.
Validate Kernel Sentinel policy constraints for evidence scope, finding quality, report size, and safe observation behavior.
Guard against empty or non-actionable Sentinel feedback by requiring usable issue evidence and repair diagnostics.
Validate installer command surfaces, wrapper names, repair behavior, and bootstrap fallback contracts across platforms.
Generate a Gateway boundary report showing whether external surfaces remain typed, bounded, and membrane-mediated.
Guard external Gateway surfaces against raw state leaks, unbudgeted routes, and direct runtime-authority exposure.
Run post-install memory-runtime setup checks needed for local operation after installer repair or source fallback.
Gate legacy runner use during release so compatibility paths stay explicit and do not become the default execution surface.
Validate release hardening-window evidence before promotion so temporary debt does not silently survive into release.
Check reviewer redundancy expectations for release review so a single weak signal does not become release confidence.
Require capability claims to carry proof burden, evidence refs, and explicit exemptions instead of assertion-only confidence.
Verify installer reliability closure evidence, including repair, wrapper availability, fallback behavior, and platform diagnostics.
Validate the unified trace substrate so one trace identity flows through evidence, tools, Sentinel, and release artifacts.
Check Nexus checkpoint policy so cross-domain handoffs remain explicit, typed, and auditable rather than implicit imports.
Detect unauthorized Kernel coupling across Nexus boundaries and preserve Kernel authority as a narrow explicit contract.
Gate release on runtime-proof evidence rather than policy-shaped claims, with explicit blockers for missing proof artifacts.
Build workspace-tooling release proof so file and search tooling remain first-class evidence alongside web tooling.
Validate Gateway support matrix coverage so adapters and platforms expose explicit status instead of vague support claims.
Guard against checked-in combined artifact shards and generated Rust bundles that inflate the repo without serving runtime truth.
